title: Add Shopify Integration Interface

requirements.text: Extend the backend SiteSettings record to include Shopify integration fields: shopifyStoreDomain (Text), shopifyStorefrontAccessToken (Text), and shopifyEnabled (Bool). Update getSiteSettings and updateSiteSettings actor methods to include these fields with sensible defaults (empty strings / false).
acceptanceCriteria: SiteSettings record includes shopifyStoreDomain, shopifyStorefrontAccessToken, and shopifyEnabled fields
getSiteSettings returns default values for these fields on first call
updateSiteSettings accepts and persists the new Shopify fields
Existing site settings fields (heroBannerImageUrl, storeName, contactEmail, address, currency) continue to work unchanged

requirements.text: Update the SiteSettings TypeScript type and the updateSiteSettings mutation payload in useQueries.ts to include the new Shopify fields: shopifyStoreDomain (string), shopifyStorefrontAccessToken (string), and shopifyEnabled (boolean).
acceptanceCriteria: SiteSettings TypeScript type includes shopifyStoreDomain, shopifyStorefrontAccessToken, and shopifyEnabled
updateSiteSettings mutation payload includes these new fields
No TypeScript type errors related to the new fields

requirements.text: Add a 'Shopify Integration' subsection to the SiteSettingsSection component in the Admin page. The subsection must include: a toggle/checkbox to enable or disable the Shopify integration (shopifyEnabled), an input field for the Shopify Store Domain (e.g. mystore.myshopify.com), and an input field for the Shopify Storefront Access Token. All fields must be loaded from getSiteSettings on mount and saved via the existing updateSiteSettings mutation. Display success/error toast notifications on save.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Shopify Integration' subsection appears in the Site Settings tab of the Admin page
An enable/disable toggle for the Shopify integration is rendered
Input fields for Shopify Store Domain and Storefront Access Token are rendered
Fields are pre-populated with current values loaded from getSiteSettings on mount
Clicking Save persists values via updateSiteSettings and shows a success toast
If save fails, an error toast is displayed

requirements.text: Add a dedicated Shopify Products Import panel within the Admin page (as a new tab or sub-section alongside Products and Site Settings). When Shopify integration is enabled and credentials are configured, display a UI that allows the admin to view a list of products fetched from the configured Shopify Storefront API (using the stored domain and access token via the Shopify Storefront GraphQL API from the frontend). Each product entry should show the product title, image thumbnail, price, and an 'Import' button. Clicking 'Import' calls the backend addProduct method to save the product to the ICP store.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Shopify Import' tab or panel appears in the Admin page when shopifyEnabled is true
When Shopify credentials are missing or integration is disabled, a prompt to configure settings is shown instead
Products are fetched from the Shopify Storefront GraphQL API using the stored domain and access token
Each product card shows title, thumbnail image, and price
Clicking 'Import' on a product card calls addProduct on the backend with the product data and shows a success or error toast
A loading state is shown while products are being fetched from Shopify

constraints: Do not break existing SiteSettings fields (heroBannerImageUrl, storeName, contactEmail, address, currency)
Shopify Storefront API calls must be made from the frontend only — do not proxy through the ICP backend actor
Immutable frontend paths (hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, hooks/useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui) must not be modified
nonGoals: Full Shopify order sync or two-way inventory sync
Shopify Admin API integration (only Storefront API)
Replacing the existing ICP product catalog with Shopify as the primary data store
